System · Story · Simulation

The Woodland System

One rendering idea, presented in three forms: a formal abstract for structured review, a story for intuition, and a live interactive layer for direct experience.

Read Abstract → Explore Story Open Observatory
Same system. Three representations: abstract → story → interactive.
Concept Map
Rendering as projection
world data exists independently of the render tree.
Boundary events
effects are triggered by interaction, not painted on top.
Three entry points
formal, intuitive, and experiential.
Narrative Layer

The story version

The same system can be explained through a short folk tale. This keeps the core idea readable without reducing it to decoration or vague metaphor.

The Woodland System

A compact narrative representation of the rendering logic: observation first, system behavior second, implementation third.

🦊 Open Story (EN) 🌲 Open Story (RU)

Technical abstract

The formal version presents the same system in a structured way: visibility-driven rendering, spatial indexing, object lifecycle, and practical extension paths.

Read Abstract →
Rendering is projection
Only visible state becomes active render state.
Visibility over existence
Off-screen entities are data, not active display objects.
Lifecycle-driven objects
Acquire → update → release, instead of brute-force creation.
Interaction-driven visuals
Effects appear as a result of thresholds, contact, and transitions.
Interactive Surface

Related live experiments

These demos show the same general thinking applied to interaction systems, presence, and visual state.

Observatory V2
Presence, echo, bloom, and constellation-like responses in a shared field.
Rendering Approach
A compact visual framing of the visibility-driven rendering idea.
Main Landing
A broader portfolio of interactive systems, experiments, and real-time demos.
Built as a minimal entry point: abstract → story → simulation.
Abstract Story Observatory Main Landing
